Day 1 — System Overview, Navigation & Setup

Welcome to Invex ERP training. Today is about getting comfortable in the system before we start running real scenarios. You will learn how to navigate the main menu, understand key concepts like CPS and PPS, and configure your preferences and filters for your specific functional group.

4h
Session
6
Groups
5
Order Types
Learning objectives
→Navigate the Invex main menu and find your functional homepage
→Understand what a CPS and PPS are and how they relate to your work
→Know the 5 order types and when each one is used
→Understand the end-to-end fulfillment workflow
→Configure your user preferences and permanent filters for your group
→Understand product coding and how materials are identified in Invex
★ Facilitator notes — Welcome (8:00–8:30)
  • Confirm everyone has system access before starting — have IT standing by
  • Walk through the main menu screenshot on screen and identify key areas for each group
  • Emphasize today is about orientation — no pressure to have everything memorized
  • Use the group filter in the sidebar to show only that group’s setup slides during setup time
Navigation & the main menu

Invex Login — access & credentials

Login · Step 1All groups
Open the URL above in Chrome or Edge. Enter your User ID (Personnel Number, leading zeros stripped) and the shared training password. Do not change the password — it is shared across all trainees in the environment.

Invex Login — select environment

Login · Step 2All groups
The environment picker appears after credentials are validated. Always choose tststw (Test Steel Warehouse) — this is the sandboxed training environment. Any other environment is live production data.

Invex Login — main menu (success)

Login · Step 3All groups
Once logged in successfully you should see the Invex main menu with the Favorites panel on the left, the tststw database label in the header, and your User ID in the top-right. If anything looks wrong, log out and reattempt before proceeding.

Invex main menu — favorites

Slide 1All groups
The Invex main menu shows your Favorites panel on the left and quick-launch tiles in the center. Each functional group has its own homepage — Sales uses the Sales Homepage, Purchasing uses the Purchasing Homepage, etc. Any screen can be added to your Favorites for faster access.
Key concepts
CPS
Customer Part Spec
A CPS (Customer Part Specification) defines exactly how a finished product must be made for a specific customer — dimensions, grade, finish, tolerances, packaging, and certifications. Every customer order ties back to a CPS. Think of it as the customer’s blueprint for a specific product.
PPS
Purchase Part Spec
A PPS (Purchase Part Specification) defines the raw material we buy from a supplier — incoming coil spec including grade, gauge, width, and quality standards. Where a CPS describes what we deliver to the customer, the PPS describes what we receive from the mill. A CPS typically references a PPS as its source material.
Job Set / IP
Planning a Job Set
A Job Set is a group of Jobs (IPs — Inventory Processing steps) required to fulfill an order item. Each IP represents one processing step in the route: Pick, Level, Package. Jobs are planned in backwards sequence from the due date. Each IP consumes and produces material, passing it to the next step.
Product Coding
How materials are coded
Products in Invex are identified by form, grade, and dimension. Coils are coded by width (48” Wide, 60” Wide). Sheets and plates are coded by dimension (60”×120”) or shape (diameter, irregular). Understanding this coding is essential for reading stock, quotes, and order items correctly.

Product coding reference

Week 1 · Slide 2
Dimension coding for coils vs. sheets/plates. Use this as a reference when reading stock screens or entering order items.

Planning a job set

Week 1 · Slide 12
A Job Set groups all the IPs (Inventory Processing steps) needed to fulfill an order. Planning runs backwards from the due date through each production step. Each IP passes its produced item to the next step in the route.
Invex order types — when to use each one

There are 5 order types in Invex. Choosing the right one determines how the system tracks material, production, and fulfillment. Click any card to learn more.

1
Regular sales order
Spot & non-contract sales
2
Blanket sales order
Contract release management
3
Source customer production order
Produce ahead for blanket
4
Release to pick
Ship from source order stock
5
Release to produce
No floor stock — produce from blanket
Regular sales order
Used for spot sales or any sale not tied into a contract. You create a spot quote, the customer awards it with a PO, and you convert it directly into a regular sales order that flows through the full fulfillment process.
Used in: Day 2 — Spot Sale scenarios.
Order fulfillment workflow

Steel Warehouse end-to-end fulfillment flow

Week 2 · Slide 18
Every order follows this sequence regardless of type. Sales Order starts the process; Invoicing ends it. Understanding where your role fits in this chain is critical to knowing what to do when something goes wrong upstream or downstream.
Initial setup — preferences & filters

Use the group filter in the sidebar to show only your group’s setup slides. Each slide shows the exact settings to configure before you start working in the system.

★ Facilitator notes — Setup (9:30–11:00)
  • Walk through each group’s setup slides together before they configure on their own
  • Use the group filter on the left to show only the relevant slides for the current group
  • Confirm every person has saved their settings before moving on — unsaved preferences reset on logout
  • Common issue: Default Branch not set — causes orders to route to the wrong location
Stock Available — user preferences
Slide 2All groups
Set these User Preferences in Stock Available before your first day of real work. Key settings: Default Whs = CHA, Wgt Disp UM = (LBS) Pounds, Totals Level = Detail.
Stock Available — permanent filters
Slide 3All groups
Configure Permanent Filters in Stock Available to pre-filter inventory to your branch and relevant product types. Save these as Permanent so they persist between sessions.
Purchasing — filters & user preferences
Slide 4Purchasing
Set your Buyer to yourself. Configure Transaction Status and PO Type filters to match your workflow. Save as Permanent.
Quote/Order entry — user preferences
Slide 5Sales
Default Branch = CHA, Default Sales Category = CS (Contract Sale), Taken-by Slp = yourself. Set Prd Ent Mthd = Customer Part Spec and Stock Disp = Stock Available.
Quote/Order entry — permanent filters
Slide 5Sales
Set your Quote/Order Entry filters to show only your branch and relevant order types. Save as Permanent so these apply every time you open the screen.
Production Planning — user preferences
Slide 6Production Planning
Production Planning preferences control how job sets and IPs display. Configure to match your production center and planning view preferences.
Job Scheduling — user preferences
Slide 7Job Scheduling
Set your default work center and date range before scheduling any jobs. These settings determine which jobs appear in your scheduling view.
Production Recording — user preferences
Slide 8Production Recording
Production Recording preferences determine which work center lineup displays and how tags are scanned. Configure before your first production recording session.
Transport Planning — filters & user preferences
Slide 9Shipping / Transport
Set your default branch and ship-from location. Use filters to focus on your destination warehouse and date range.
INVEXops Mobile — user preferences
Slide 10All groups
INVEXops Mobile is used for production recording and receiving on the floor. Configure your work center and location defaults before using the mobile app.
Invoice Entry — filters & user preferences
Slide 11Sales
Invoice Entry preferences control which shipments display for invoicing. Configure your branch and salesperson defaults to ensure only your orders appear in the invoice queue.
End of day debrief
★ Facilitator notes — Debrief (12:30–1:00)
  • Have everyone log out and back in to verify settings persisted
  • Quick verbal check: can everyone find their functional homepage? Can everyone open Stock Available?
  • Address any access issues before Day 2 — escalate to IT immediately
  • Preview Day 2: Spot Sale — trainees will run a real spot quote end-to-end
  • Remind trainees to bring their user ID and role code to every session
All trainees logged into Invex successfully
User preferences saved and verified for each group
Permanent filters set and saved
Everyone can navigate to their functional homepage
CPS and PPS concepts understood — questions answered
5 order types reviewed — everyone knows the difference between a regular SO and a blanket
Open access issues escalated to IT
Day 2 — Spot Sale previewed

Spot Sale — End-to-End Process

Today you will practice creating spot quotes, managing line items, handling awarded and non-awarded lines, converting to sales orders, and following the full process through shipping and invoicing in Invex.

3
Scenarios
4h
Session
47
Unhappy Paths
6
Process Levels
Learning objectives
→Create a spot quote with single and multiple line items
→Disposition non-awarded items (lost — price)
→Convert awarded lines to a sales order
→Follow the order through purchasing, receiving, production, shipping and invoicing
→Handle special processing: blast, level & shear, slit & level
★ Facilitator notes — Intro (8:00–8:30)
  • Walk through the Order Management process map (L1) on screen before starting scenarios
  • Emphasize difference between a spot quote and a blanket/contract order
  • Remind trainees: Scribe instructions are embedded in each activity in the process map — click any activity to see step-by-step instructions
  • Confirm everyone is logged into Invex and has access before starting Beginner scenario
Day 1 Scenarios
Beginner
Single item spot sale — Siskin
Siskin Steel
Not started
▼
Line items
  • 1/4" 60×120 HR Sheets — 45,000 lbs Awarded
Scenario flow
  • Customer sends PO for all items
  • Convert quote to sales order
  • Follow through purchasing → receiving → production → shipping → invoicing
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Spot Sale ↗ Create Quote ↗ Convert to SO ↗ Create CPS ↗ Disposition
Step checklist
Receive spot request from customer
Sales
Create spot quote in Invex
Sales
Add line item: 1/4″ 60×120 HR — 45k lbs
Sales
Submit quote to customer
Sales
Receive customer PO — line awarded
Sales
Convert quote to sales order
Sales
Create CPS — confirm finished product specifications
Sales
Create purchase requisition for 1/4″ 60×120 HR — 45k lbs
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive coil into destination warehouse
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job set
Production Planning
Schedule job on work center
Job Scheduling
Process & level material per process route
Production Recording
Package & tag finished output
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create transport order
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date
Shipping
Create invoice
Sales
Open Process Tracker

Log confirmation numbers and time each step in the process tracker.

Intermediate
Multi-item with partial award — Triple S
Triple S Steel
Not started
▼
Line items
  • 3/8" 60×96 HRPO Sheets — 20,000 lbs Awarded
  • 1/4" 72×120 HR Sheets — 25,000 lbs Awarded
  • 3/4" 60×120 HR Sheets — 30,000 lbs Lost — Price
Key actions
  • Disposition 3/4" line as Lost — Price
  • Convert 3/8" and 1/4" lines to sales order
  • Follow both awarded lines to invoicing
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Spot Sale ↗ Create Quote ↗ Convert to SO ↗ Disposition
Step checklist
Receive spot request from customer
Sales
Create spot quote — add all 3 line items
Sales
Receive partial PO (3/8" and 1/4" only)
Sales
Disposition 3/4" line as Lost — Price
Sales
Convert awarded lines to sales order
Sales
Create CPS for each awarded line
Sales
Create purchase requisition for both awarded lines
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive both coils into destination warehouse
Receiving
Assign material to both orders & create job sets
Production Planning
Schedule jobs on work center
Job Scheduling
Process & level both lines per process routes
Production Recording
Package & tag finished output for both lines
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create transport order
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date
Shipping
Invoice both lines
Sales

Advanced
Multi-item with special processing — Siskin
Siskin Steel
Not started
▼
Line items
  • 3/8" 60×96 HR Blasted — 30,000 lbs Awarded Blast
  • 1/4" 58.5×120 HR — 25,000 lbs Awarded Level & Shear
  • 1/4" 5×120 HR — 10,000 lbs Awarded Slit & Level
Special processing notes
  • Blast — apply blast flag to 3/8" line
  • Level & Shear — 58.5" width requires level and shear op
  • Slit & Level — 5" width slit from master coil then leveled
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Spot Sale ↗ Create Quote ↗ Convert to SO ↗ Create CPS ↗ Disposition
Step checklist
Receive spot request from customer
Sales
Create spot quote — add all 3 lines with special processing flags
Sales
Configure blast processing on 3/8" line
Sales
Configure level & shear on 1/4" 58.5×120
Sales
Configure slit & level on 1/4" 5×120
Sales
Customer PO received — all 3 lines awarded
Sales
Convert to sales order
Sales
Create CPS — 3 separate CPSs with special processing routes
Sales
Create purchase requisition for all 3 lines
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive all coils into destination warehouse
Receiving
Assign material to all 3 orders & create job sets
Production Planning
Schedule jobs — sequence blast, level/shear, slit/level
Job Scheduling
Run production — blast, level/shear, slit/level ops
Production Recording
Package & tag finished output for all 3 lines
Production Recording
Move all finished material to storage locations
Inventory
Create transport order
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date
Shipping
Invoice all 3 lines
Sales

Supplemental Exercises — Extra Practice
Finished early? Each card below is a real Siskin Steel customer PO. Practice entering it as a spot sale in Invex — create the quote, add every line item exactly as shown, match the pricing and delivery date, then convert to a sales order and note the SO# in the tracker. Pay attention to special processing requirements and non-standard sizes.
Exercise 1
Spot Sale entry — Siskin Steel PO #168764
Siskin Steel & Supply Co. — 2 line items — due 04/14/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — identify both line items, delivery date, and special instructions
Sales
Create spot quote in Invex — customer: Siskin Steel & Supply
Sales
Add Line 1: A36 1/4" × 60 × 120 — 65 PCS — $54.40/CWT — Code 441044
Sales
Add Line 2: A36 1/4" × 60 × 288 — 10 PCS — $54.40/CWT — Code 441047 (non-standard 24 ft length — flag for special handling)
Sales
Confirm delivery date: 04/14/26 — FOB Destination — Freight Prepaid
Sales
Note mill cert requirement on order (Surface & Flatness Critical)
Sales
Convert quote to sales order — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition for both lines — coil or plate stock
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive coils into destination warehouse — verify against purchase order
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job set
Production Planning
Schedule & run production job — prioritize to meet 04/14/26 delivery
Job Scheduling
Cut material to specified dimensions — note 288″ (24 ft) length on Line 2 requires special handling — tag finished pieces
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to Siskin Steel, 1901 Riverfront Pkwy, Chattanooga, TN
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date — verify piece count before loading
Shipping
Invoice the shipment — log invoice# in tracker
Sales

Exercise 3
Spot Sale entry — Siskin Steel PO #168846
Siskin Steel & Supply Co. — 11 line items — mixed grades & gauges — due 04/20/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — note 3 different grades (A36, A572 Gr50), mix of plates and HR sheets, 3 color codes (Yellow, Black, Green)
Sales
Create spot quote in Invex — customer: Siskin Steel & Supply
Sales
Add Line 1: A36 1/4" × 72 × 240 — 45 PCS — $55.50/CWT — Code 441056
Sales
Add Line 2: A572 Gr50 1/4" × 60 × 120 (Yellow) — 45 PCS — $57.00/CWT — Code 441544
Sales
Add Line 3: A572 Gr50 HSLA 3/8" × 60 × 120 (Yellow) — 30 PCS — $57.00/CWT — Code 443546
Sales
Add Line 4: A36 1/2" × 60 × 120 — 40 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 444040
Sales
Add Line 5: A36 1/2" × 60 × 240 — 5 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 444046
Sales
Add Line 6: HR Sheet A1011 CS (Black) 10 GA 48" × 96" — 55 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 601332
Sales
Add Line 7: HR Sheet A1011 CS (Black) 10 GA 48 × 120 — 45 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 601334
Sales
Add Line 8: HR Sheet A1011 CS (Black) 10 GA 60 × 120 — 75 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 601344
Sales
Add Line 9: HR Sheet A1011 CS (Green) 12 GA 60 × 120 — 95 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 603044
Sales
Add Line 10: HR Sheet A1011 CS (Green) 12 GA 60 × 144 — 25 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 603045
Sales
Add Line 11: HR Sheet A1011 CS (Green) 12 GA 72 × 144 — 65 PCS — $55.00/CWT — Code 603055
Sales
Confirm all 11 lines match PO exactly — delivery date 04/20/26 on all lines
Sales
Convert quote to sales order — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition for all 11 lines — multiple grades (A36, A572 Gr50, A1011) and gauges require separate sourcing
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive all coils into destination warehouse — verify grade, gauge, and color code against purchase orders
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job sets — may require multiple job sets for different grades
Production Planning
Schedule & run all production jobs — coordinate multiple lines to meet 04/20/26 delivery
Job Scheduling
Process all 11 lines — verify grade and color code per line (Yellow/Black/Green HR sheets) — tag all finished pieces
Production Recording
Move all finished material to storage locations — stage by line item for shipping efficiency
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to Siskin Steel, 1901 Riverfront Pkwy, Chattanooga, TN — likely multiple loads given total piece count
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date — verify all 11 line items accounted for before departure
Shipping
Invoice the shipment(s) — log invoice# in tracker
Sales

Spot Sale entry — GHE Metal Solutions PO #140896
GH Solutions — 1 line item — Fort Payne, AL — due 02/12/26 — note price format & payment terms
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — note price is shown as $/lb ($0.52950 = $52.95/CWT), FOB is Fort Payne (SW pays freight), and terms are 1/2% 10 net 30
Sales
Create spot quote in Invex — customer: GH Solutions (Fort Payne, AL)
Sales
Add Line 1: A1011 CS .119" (10 GA) × 60 × 120 P&O — 175 PCS — $52.95/CWT — ship to Fort Payne
Sales
Set FOB: Fort Payne, AL — Ship Via: Vendor Truck — Terms: 1/2% 10, net 30
Sales
Note mill cert / conformance requirement on order
Sales
Confirm delivery date: 02/12/26 — only 6 days from PO date, flag if capacity is tight
Sales
Convert quote to sales order — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition — 10GA P&O HR sheet coil or cut-to-size stock to fill 175 pcs
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive coil into destination warehouse — verify gauge, finish, and quantity against purchase order
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job set
Production Planning
Schedule & run production job — tight window, confirm capacity before committing
Job Scheduling
Process A1011 CS .119″ P&O sheet to 60×120 — verify surface quality — tag all 175 pieces
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to GH Solutions, 3709 Williams Ave NE, Fort Payne, AL — SW pays freight (FOB Fort Payne)
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date — include conformance documentation with shipment
Shipping
Invoice the shipment — log invoice# in tracker

Unhappy Path Reference — All 47 Scenarios
Training mode Awareness mode shows context + resolution guidance for discussion. Practice mode adds resolution checklists for hands-on training.
47 of 47
Practice Games
Memory Match — Day 2 Spot Sale

Process Sequence — Day 2 Spot Sale
Drag (or tap twice) to reorder these steps into the correct process sequence, then click Check Order.
Spot the Error — Day 2 Spot Sale
Read the sales order below. Click any field you think contains an error. When you’re done, click Check Answers.
Department Sort — Day 2 Spot Sale
Drag each step into the department responsible for it. On mobile: tap a step to select it (highlighted), then tap a column to place it. Click Check Answers when all steps are placed.
Term Blaster — Day 2 Spot Sale
Press Start to play
TERM BLASTER
Day 2 — Spot Sale
Read the definition below.

A / D or ←→ to move  •  Space to fire

The definition will appear here when the game starts.
End of Day Debrief
★ Facilitator notes — Debrief (12:30–1:00)
  • Export CSVs from the process tracker — review elapsed times as a group, identify which steps took longest and why
  • Ask trainees: what was the hardest part? What would make it faster?
  • Review 2–3 unhappy paths as discussion items — don't try to cover all 47 today
  • Log any open system issues or questions for IT / project team follow-up
  • Preview Day 2: Contract Sale / Blanket Orders
All 3 scenarios attempted by each trainee
Process tracker CSVs exported and saved
2–3 unhappy paths discussed as a group
Open questions documented and sent to project team
System access issues resolved or escalated
Day 2 topics previewed (Contract Sale / Blanket Orders)
Trainee feedback collected

All Process Maps ►
Intro
8:00–8:30
Beginner
8:30–9:30
Break
9:30–9:45
Intermediate
9:45–11:15
Advanced
11:15–12:30
Debrief
12:30–1:00

Contract Sale — Blanket Orders & Source Orders

Today you will practice the full contract sale process: creating blanket orders, building source orders to pre-produce inventory, creating releases to pick from on-hand stock, and creating releases to produce when no floor stock exists.

3
Scenarios
4h
Session
47
Unhappy Paths
6
Process Levels
Learning objectives
→Create a blanket order with single and multiple line items
→Create a source order to produce material ahead of demand
→Create a release to pick from existing source order inventory
→Create a release to produce when no floor stock is available
→Handle multiple production routes: Level/Blast, Slit/Level, Level/Shear, Level/Temper
★ Facilitator notes — Intro (8:00–8:30)
  • Walk through the Contract Sale process map (L2) on screen before starting scenarios
  • Emphasize the difference between a source order (produce ahead of demand) vs. a release to produce (produce on demand from the blanket)
  • Clarify when to create a release to pick (inventory already on the floor from a source order) vs. a release to produce (no pre-built stock)
  • Confirm everyone is logged into Invex and has access before starting the Beginner scenario
Day 3 Scenarios
Beginner
Single item blanket order with source order — Kubota
Kubota
Not started
▼
Blanket order
  • 3/16" 60×120 HR Sheets — 90,000 lbs Contracted
Source order
  • Purchase 70k# coil
  • Create source order → produce from coil (Level/Temper route)
Release
  • Create release to pick — 1 truckload (45,000 lbs)
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Contract Sale ↗ Blanket Order ↗ Source Order ↗ Release to Pick
Step checklist
Create blanket order for Kubota — 1 line item
Sales
Add line: 3/16" 60×120 HR — 90k# contracted qty
Sales
Create purchase requisition for coil — 70k#
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when coil arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive coil into destination warehouse
Receiving
Create source order (Level/Temper route)
Production Planning
Schedule & run production job
Job Scheduling
Record production — material confirmed on floor
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create release to pick — 1 truckload (45k#)
Sales
Create transport order
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date
Shipping
Invoice the shipment
Sales

Intermediate
Multi-length blanket order with release to produce — Interstate Sheet
Interstate Sheet
Not started
▼
Blanket order — same product, 3 lengths
  • 1/4" 60×96 HR Sheets — 30,000 lbs Contracted
  • 1/4" 60×120 HR Sheets — 30,000 lbs Contracted
  • 1/4" 60×144 HR Sheets — 30,000 lbs Contracted
Key actions
  • No source order — planning to produce as needed, not ahead of demand
  • Create release to produce — Truckload 1 (mix of 3 items, ~45k#)
  • Create release to produce — Truckload 2 (mix of 3 items, ~45k#)
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Contract Sale ↗ Blanket Order ↗ Release to Produce
Step checklist
Create blanket order for Interstate Sheet — 3 line items
Sales
Add line 1: 1/4" 60×96 HR — 30k#
Sales
Add line 2: 1/4" 60×120 HR — 30k#
Sales
Add line 3: 1/4" 60×144 HR — 30k#
Sales
Create purchase requisition for coil — 70k# (no source order created)
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when coil arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive coil into destination warehouse
Receiving
Create release to produce — Truckload 1 (mix, ~45k#)
Production Planning
Create release to produce — Truckload 2 (mix, ~45k#)
Production Planning
Schedule & run production for both releases
Job Scheduling
Record production output for both truckloads
Production Recording
Move material to designated storage locations
Inventory
Create transport order — Truckload 1
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior — Truckload 1
Shipping
Load & ship Truckload 1 on transport date
Shipping
Create transport order — Truckload 2
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior — Truckload 2
Shipping
Load & ship Truckload 2 on transport date
Shipping
Invoice both shipments
Sales

Log confirmation numbers and time each step in the process tracker.

Open Tracker ↗
Advanced
Multi-product blanket with mixed source orders — Kubota
Kubota
Not started
▼
Blanket order — 5 products, 90k# each (purchase: 70k# coils)
  • 3/16" 60×120 HR Blasted — 90,000 lbs Level/Blast
  • 1/4" 5×120 HR Slit — 90,000 lbs Slit/Level
  • 1/4" 58.5×120 HR Sheared — 90,000 lbs Level/Shear
  • 3/8" 60×96 HR Tempered — 90,000 lbs Level/Temper
  • 1/2" 60×120 HR Tempered — 90,000 lbs Level/Temper
Source order details
  • P1 Level/Blast — 70k# coil → cut 45k# to source order, 25k# to floor stock
  • P2 Slit/Level — 70k# coil → cut all 70k# to source order (overproduce to order)
  • P3 Level/Shear — locate existing sheets on the floor, shear to size (no new coil purchase)
  • P4 Level/Temper — 70k# coil → cut 45k# to source order, 25k# to floor stock
  • P5 Level/Temper — 70k# coil → cut 45k# to source order, 25k# to floor stock
Release to pick
  • Create 3 truckloads of mixed material picked from source order inventory
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Contract Sale ↗ Blanket Order ↗ Source Order ↗ Release to Pick
Step checklist
Create blanket order for Kubota — 5 products, 90k# each
Sales
Add P1: 3/16" 60×120 HR Blasted — 90k# (Level/Blast)
Sales
Add P2: 1/4" 5×120 HR Slit — 90k# (Slit/Level)
Sales
Add P3: 1/4" 58.5×120 HR Sheared — 90k# (Level/Shear)
Sales
Add P4: 3/8" 60×96 HR Tempered — 90k# (Level/Temper)
Sales
Add P5: 1/2" 60×120 HR Tempered — 90k# (Level/Temper)
Sales
Create purchase requisition for 4 coils × 70k# (P1, P2, P4, P5)
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when coils arrive
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive all 4 coils into destination warehouse
Receiving
Create source order — P1 (Level/Blast, 70k# coil)
Production Planning
Create source order — P2 (Slit/Level, 70k# coil)
Production Planning
Create source order — P3 (Level/Shear, use floor sheets)
Production Planning
Create source order — P4 (Level/Temper, 70k# coil)
Production Planning
Create source order — P5 (Level/Temper, 70k# coil)
Production Planning
Run Level/Blast job — cut 45k# to order, 25k# to floor stock
Job Scheduling
Run Slit/Level job — cut all 70k# to source order
Job Scheduling
Run Level/Shear job — pick floor sheets, shear to size
Job Scheduling
Run Level/Temper job — P4: cut 45k# to order, 25k# to stock
Job Scheduling
Run Level/Temper job — P5: cut 45k# to order, 25k# to stock
Job Scheduling
Record production — all 5 source orders
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage locations
Inventory
Create release to pick — Truckload 1 (mixed material)
Sales
Create release to pick — Truckload 2 (mixed material)
Sales
Create release to pick — Truckload 3 (mixed material)
Sales
Create transport orders — all 3 truckloads
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship all 3 truckloads on transport date
Shipping
Invoice all shipments
Sales
Open Process Tracker

Log confirmation numbers and time each step in the process tracker.

Open Tracker ↗
Supplemental Exercises — Extra Practice
Finished early? Each card below is a real customer PO for a contract sale. Exercises 1 & 2: require creating a blanket order in Invex first — the job numbers and release quantities are the cue that a blanket framework must exist before you enter individual release lines. Exercise 3 (Advanced Steel Technology): straight contract sale — no blanket needed — but tests metric dimension entry (all specs in mm and kg), Toyota Purchasing Program compliance, 5-item stenciling requirements, and appointment-only delivery logistics. Exercises 4–6 (Sheet Metal Components): multi-line HRPO sheet & plate orders priced per EA (each piece) — tests mixed gauge and grade entry (A36, A572 GR50, A1011 CS, HSLA/A715 GR50) across 6–7 line items with tight lead times. Exercises 7 & 8 (SMF): repeat contract plate orders — tests LB pricing, mixed metric/imperial spec entry (10MM thickness with inch width & length), customer part number cross-reference, and price-change awareness on a repeat order. Exercises 9 & 10 (Interstate Sheet Metal): TP100 abrasion-resistant specialty plate orders — tests specialty product sourcing (TP100/AR100 vs. standard grades), mixed unit-of-measure entry (PC, EA, SH on same PO), non-standard cut sizes, feet-to-inch dimension conversion, FOB Delivered freight handling, .5%10 N30 early-pay terms, and MTR compliance on all line items. Exercise 10 adds a mixed-product challenge: TP100 and standard GR50 HRPO sheet on the same order.
Exercise 1
Contract Sale entry — Taylor Manufacturing PO #50800
Taylor Manufacturing — 2 lines — metric plate, shot blasted — priced per EA — due 03/05/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Create blanket order first — Taylor Manufacturing requires a blanket framework in Invex before releases can be entered against it
Sales
Review PO — note pricing is per EA (not $/CWT), dimensions are metric (MM), processing is Shot Blasted (A13C spec), and pieces are allocated to specific job numbers
Sales
Set FOB: Chattanooga, TN — Ship Via: Best Way — Terms: Net 30 — Contact: Angela Burnett / William Hatfield
Sales
Enter Line 1 release: PLT-19MM-60X120-A13C — 6 pcs — $790.82 EA — due 03/05/26
Job allocation: 99395-01 (5 pcs), STOCK (1 pc)
Sales
Enter Line 2 release: PLT-25MM-72X160-A13C — 11 pcs — $2,024.79 EA — due 03/05/26
Job allocation: 99263-08 (2.75), 99263-09 (3), 99263-27 (0.25), 99477-01 (3), 99477-02 (2)
Sales
Note mill cert requirement: submit to Mill.Certs@taylormfg.net — Taylor PO # must be on cert
Sales
Verify order total: $27,017.61 — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition for plate material — 19MM and 25MM metric dimensions — confirm sourcing for both sizes
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive plate into destination warehouse — verify metric dimensions against purchase order
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job set — note job allocations across multiple Taylor job numbers (99395-01, 99263-08/09/27, 99477-01/02)
Production Planning
Schedule & run production job — due 03/05/26
Job Scheduling
Run shot blast to A13C spec — tag all 17 pieces per job allocation: Line 1 (5 pcs job 99395-01, 1 pc stock), Line 2 (per job numbers 99263-08/09/27, 99477-01/02)
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to Taylor Manufacturing, 224 Adams Street, Stevenson, AL 35772
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment — confirm mill cert is prepared for shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date — include mill cert with shipment
Shipping
Email mill cert to Mill.Certs@taylormfg.net with Taylor PO# listed — invoice the shipment — log invoice# in tracker
Sales

Exercise 2
Contract Sale entry — O’Neal Manufacturing Services PO #427713
O’Neal Manufacturing Services — 1 release line — 45,000 LB — metric P&O plate — multi-location billing — due 02/28/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Create blanket order first — the Release structure on this PO confirms it is a release against an O’Neal blanket framework — blanket must exist in Invex before the release can be entered
Sales
Review PO — note quantity is in pounds (LB), price is $0.62750/lb, metric thickness (14.0MM), 72″ × 249″ (nearly 21 ft — non-standard), “Direct Buy CAT” designation, and three different address locations (supplier, ship-to, invoice-to)
Sales
Supplier is listed as South Bend #774005 — confirm which SW branch is fulfilling and whether ship-to (Greensboro, NC) affects routing
Sales
Set Ship Via: Common Carrier — FOB: Destination — Freight: Prepaid — Terms: .5% 10, Net 37
Sales
Enter release: PLHR.551-002-DB-CAT — Plate HR 14.0MM P&O 72″ × 249″ — 45,000 LB — $0.62750/lb — due 02/28/26 — ship to OMS-Greensboro
Sales
Note delivery requirements: appointment only — call 336-664-6485 — loads must be TARPED — PPE required at plant
Sales
Note MTR requirement: O’Neal PO# and item# must appear on MTR — MTRs must accompany truck shipment — invoice to Birmingham, AL office
Sales
Verify PO total: $28,237.50 — log blanket SO# and release SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition for wide-format plate — 14.0MM P&O, 72″ × 249″ (nearly 21 ft) — confirm sourcing for non-standard dimensions
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive 45,000 LB into destination warehouse — verify weight, thickness (14.0MM), and P&O finish against purchase order
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job set
Production Planning
Schedule & run production job — due 02/28/26
Job Scheduling
Cut plate to 72″ × 249″ dimensions — verify 14.0MM P&O finish — confirm equipment capacity for 21-ft length — tag finished pieces
Production Recording
Move finished material to storage location
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to OMS-Greensboro, 105 E Florida St, Greensboro, NC 27406 — delivery by appointment (call 336-664-6485)
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior — confirm TARPED loads — ensure MTRs prepared with O’Neal PO# and item#
Shipping
Load & ship on transport date — ALL loads TARPED — MTRs must accompany shipment
Shipping
Invoice to Birmingham, AL office (OMS-invoices@onealmfg.com) — O’Neal PO# on all documents — log invoice# in tracker
Sales

Exercise 3
Contract Sale entry — Advanced Steel Technology #ASWT2606
Advanced Steel Technology — 52 pcs A36 plate — all dimensions in METRIC (mm/kg) — Toyota Purchasing Program — 5-item paint stencil — flatbed/appt required — due 06/05/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — contract sale — Toyota Purchasing Program customer — critical: ALL dimensions are in METRIC (mm and kg) — enter line items using the metric specs exactly as written — 16mm gauge = 0.630″ and 19mm gauge = 0.750″ (reference only — enter metric)
Sales
AST Contact: Leanne Godbee (lgodbee@adv-steel.com, 1-706-232-5877 x128) — Delivery/logistics: logistics@adv-steel.com (must email 48 hrs in advance for appointment) — SWC Contact: Brian Micinski (Brian.Micinski@steelwarehouse.net, 423-697-0925)
Sales
Enter sales order PO# ASWT2606 — Line 1: 36 pcs A36, 16mm × 1422mm × 5969mm, 38,379 kg, CWT $54.71, due 06/05/2026 — Line 2: 16 pcs A36, 19mm × 1422mm × 5639mm, 19,136 kg, CWT $61.19, due 06/05/2026
Sales
Note: qty is based on tonnage yield — must confirm actual piece count with AST when ready to ship — note: price is paid on theoretical weight — material must meet AST and half ASTM A6/A6M standards (thickness, flatness, squareness) — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition for A36 plate meeting half ASTM A6/A6M requirements — source 16mm and 19mm at 1422mm width — confirm mill cert will include metric dimensions, melt/heat number, and mill name
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse
Purchasing
Receive plate into destination warehouse — verify metric dimensions (16mm or 19mm thickness, 1422mm width) and A36 grade against purchase order
Receiving
Assign material to order — create job set — plan CTL to metric lengths: 5969mm (Line 1, 16mm) and 5639mm (Line 2, 19mm)
Production Planning
Schedule CTL job — due 06/05/2026 — do NOT ship before June 2026 without written permission from AST
Job Scheduling
Cut plates to metric specs — 36 pcs at 16mm×1422mm×5969mm and 16 pcs at 19mm×1422mm×5639mm — STENCIL ALL PIECES WITH PAINT (DO NOT STAMP) — in 3 places per piece if possible: (1) AST PO# ASWT2606, (2) Metric size, (3) Grade A36, (4) Melt/heat number, (5) Name of mill
Production Recording
Move finished plates to staging — count actual pieces (qty is based on tonnage yield) — confirm piece count with AST before shipping — do not ship without AST confirmation
Inventory
EMAIL logistics@adv-steel.com at least 48 hours in advance to book delivery appointment — confirm piece count in email — APPOINTMENT ONLY — Monday–Friday only — NO CONESTOGAS — FLATBED required — material must be tarped on all flatbeds until authorized to remove after check-in
Sales
Stage on FLATBED only day prior — TARP ALL loads — no dunnage or strapping required (AST unloads with overhead magnetic crane) — DO NOT mix load PO material without AST permission
Shipping
Load & ship on transport date — tarp authorized to remove after check-in — ship to Advanced Steel Technology, 300 West Hermitage Rd NE, Rome, GA 30161
Shipping
Invoice AST based on theoretical weight — PO# ASWT2606 on invoice — submit to Bill To: Advanced Steel Technology, 300 West Hermitage Rd, Rome, GA 30161 — log invoice# in tracker
Sales
Customer PO — Advanced Steel Technology #ASWT2606

Exercise 4
Contract Sale entry — Sheet Metal Components PO #54135
Sheet Metal Components — 6 lines — HRPO plate & sheet — priced per EA — A36, A572 GR50, A1011 CS — due 04/13/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — note pricing is per EA (each piece), not $/CWT or $/LB — 6 line items at different thicknesses, gauges, and grades — PO date 04/08/26, due 04/13/26 (5-day lead time)
Sales
Note product type mix: Lines 1–5 are fractional plate (.500″, .375″, .312″, .250″); Line 6 is 11 gauge sheet (A1011 CS) — ensure correct product class is selected in Invex for each line — plate and sheet are separate product categories
Sales
Note grade complexity: Lines 1–3 and Line 5 are A36; Line 4 is A572 GR50 (high-strength) — same .250″ thickness as Line 5 but different grade and higher price — Line 6 is A1011 CS commercial sheet
Sales
Enter Line 1: .500_HRPO_A36_60X120 — 4 ea — $574.49 EA — $2,297.96
Sales
Enter Line 2: .375_HRPO_A36_48X96 — 9 ea — $276.74 EA — $2,490.66 — 48″×96″ cut size (differs from other lines)
Sales
Enter Line 3: .312_HRPO_A36_48X96 — 10 ea — $230.61 EA — $2,306.10 — 48″×96″ cut size
Sales
Enter Line 4: .250_HRPO_A572_GR50_60X120 — 18 ea — $293.37 EA — $5,280.66 — high-strength plate: A572 GR50 — do not substitute A36
Sales
Enter Line 5: .250_HRPO_A36_60X120 — 18 ea — $285.77 EA — $5,143.86 — same thickness & size as L4, different grade — $7.60/EA price difference reflects grade premium
Sales
Enter Line 6: 11GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 57 ea — $136.71 EA — $7,792.47 — largest line by value and piece count; sheet class, not plate
Sales
Acct Mgr: Matt Blentlinger — Vendor: Steel Warehouse #774005 — Terms: NET 35 — Verify order total: $25,311.71 — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisitions — separate for plate (L1–L5) and sheet (L6) — A572 GR50 (L4) requires grade-specific sourcing separate from A36 — confirm availability for 5-day lead time
Purchasing
Approve requisitions
Purchasing
Convert requisitions to POs — issue to mill or warehouse stock
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt, receipt, and transfer as needed
Purchasing
Receive all 116 total pieces — verify gauge, grade, and cut size per line — do not accept A36 in place of A572 GR50 for Line 4 — verify 48″×96″ for L2/L3 and 60″×120″ for L1/L4/L5/L6
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job sets
Production Planning
Schedule & run production — due 04/13/26
Job Scheduling
Verify cut sizes: 48″×96″ (L2, L3) and 60″×120″ (L1, L4, L5, L6) — tag pieces by line and grade — keep A572 GR50 (L4) and A36 (L5) separated — 57 pcs of 11GA sheet processed separately
Production Recording
Move finished material to staging — group by line item for loading
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to Sheet Metal Components — due 04/13/26
Sales
Stage & load all 116 pieces — count per line against PO before loading — do not mix A572 GR50 and A36 material
Shipping
Invoice shipment — Terms: NET 35 — log invoice# in tracker
Sales

Exercise 5
Contract Sale entry — Sheet Metal Components PO #54142
Sheet Metal Components — 7 lines — HRPO plate & sheet — priced per EA — A36, A572 GR50, A1011 CS, HSLA GR50 — due 04/15/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — 7-line order from Sheet Metal Components, PO date 04/09/26, due 04/15/26 — all priced per EA — introduces 7 GA gauge sheet and HSLA GR50 material not seen on PO #54135
Sales
Note grade complexity across 7 lines: Line 1 is .312″ A572 GR50 plate (high-strength); Lines 2–3 are A36 plate; Lines 4/6/7 are gauge sheet (7GA and 12GA A1011 CS commercial); Line 5 is 7GA HSLA GR50 — same gauge as L4 but different grade at a premium price — three distinct grade families on one PO
Sales
Enter Line 1: .312_HRPO_A572_GR50_60X120 — 14 ea — $366.72 EA — $5,134.08 — high-strength plate, A572 GR50
Sales
Enter Line 2: .250_HRPO_A36_60X120 — 9 ea — $285.77 EA — $2,571.93
Sales
Enter Line 3: .188_HRPO_A36_60X120 — 13 ea — $214.33 EA — $2,786.29
Sales
Enter Line 4: 7GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 26 ea — $204.95 EA — $5,328.70
Sales
Enter Line 5: 7GA_HRPO_HSLA_60X120 (HSLA GR50) — 13 ea — $210.41 EA — $2,735.33 — same gauge as L4 — higher-strength grade commands $5.46/EA premium over A1011 CS
Sales
Enter Line 6: 11GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 38 ea — $136.71 EA — $5,194.98
Sales
Enter Line 7: 12GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 22 ea — $119.56 EA — $2,630.32
Sales
Terms: NET 35 — Verify order total: $26,381.63 — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisitions — plate reqs (L1–L3) and sheet reqs (L4–L7) — A572 GR50 plate and HSLA GR50 sheet require grade-specific sourcing; do not source A36/A1011 CS as substitutes
Purchasing
Approve requisitions
Purchasing
Convert requisitions to POs — issue to mill or warehouse stock
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt, receipt, and transfer as needed

Receive all 135 total pieces — verify gauge, grade, and cut size per line — critical: do not substitute A36 for A572 GR50 (L1) or A1011 CS for HSLA GR50 (L5) — all cut sizes are 60″×120″ on this PO
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job sets
Production Planning
Schedule & run production — due 04/15/26
Job Scheduling
Process material — all 7 lines cut to 60″×120″ — tag pieces by line and grade to prevent mixing — keep HSLA GR50 (L5) and A1011 CS (L4) separate despite same gauge
Production Recording
Move finished material to staging — group by line item
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to Sheet Metal Components — due 04/15/26
Sales
Stage & load all 135 pcs — count per line against PO — do not mix grade families on load
Shipping
Invoice shipment — Terms: NET 35 — log invoice# in tracker

Exercise 6
Contract Sale entry — Sheet Metal Components PO #54161
Sheet Metal Components — 7 lines — HRPO plate & sheet — priced per EA — A36, A572 GR50, A1011 CS, A715 GR50 — due 04/21/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — 7-line order, PO date 04/15/26, due 04/21/26 — all priced per EA — note Line 7 specifies A715 GR50 (HSLA family, different ASTM spec from A1011 CS at same gauge) — do not substitute A1011 CS
Sales
Note product type mix: Lines 1–3 are fractional plate (.375″, .250″, .188″); Lines 4–7 are gauge sheet (7GA, 10GA, 11GA) — note Line 1 cut size is 48″×96″ while all other lines are 60″×120″
Sales
Enter Line 1: .375_HRPO_A36_48X96 — 9 ea — $276.74 EA — $2,490.66 — 48″×96″ cut size (only line on this PO not 60″×120″)
Sales
Enter Line 2: .250_HRPO_A572_GR50_60X120 — 9 ea — $293.37 EA — $2,640.33 — high-strength plate
Sales
Enter Line 3: .188_HRPO_A36_60X120 — 12 ea — $214.33 EA — $2,571.96
Sales
Enter Line 4: 7GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 13 ea — $204.95 EA — $2,664.35
Sales
Enter Line 5: 10GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 34 ea — $153.74 EA — $5,227.16 — largest piece-count line
Sales
Enter Line 6: 11GA_HRPO_A1011CS_60X120 — 38 ea — $136.71 EA — $5,194.98
Sales
Enter Line 7: 11GA_HRPO_HSLA_G50 (A715 GR50) — 18 ea — $140.35 EA — $2,526.30 — same gauge as L6 — A715 GR50 HSLA at $3.64/EA premium over A1011 CS
Sales
Terms: NET 35 — Verify order total: $23,315.74 — log SO# in tracker

Create purchase requisitions — plate reqs (L1–L3) and sheet reqs (L4–L7) — A572 GR50 (L2) and A715 GR50 (L7) require separate grade-specific sourcing

Create pre-receipt, receipt, and transfer as needed
Purchasing
Receive all 133 total pieces — verify gauge, grade, and cut size — Line 1 is 48″×96″; all others are 60″×120″ — keep A715 GR50 (L7) separate from A1011 CS (L4–L6) at same 11GA gauge
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job sets
Production Planning
Schedule & run production — due 04/21/26
Job Scheduling
Process material — note L1 cut size (48″×96″) differs from all other lines (60″×120″) — tag A715 GR50 pieces (L7) separately from A1011 CS pieces (L4–L6) despite same 11GA gauge

Move finished material to staging — group by line item
Inventory
Create transport order — ship to Sheet Metal Components — due 04/21/26

Contract Sale entry — SMF PO #75276
SMF — 1 line — 48,000 LB — 10MM HR plate, 64″×189″ — LB pricing — customer part# MMP101E0170 — ship to Anderson, SC — due 08/05/25
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — customer is SMF, buyer Michael Massey (309-432-2586) — single line, 48,000 LB of 10MM HR plate — note mixed metric/imperial spec: thickness is 10MM but width/length are in inches (64″×189″) — cross-reference customer part# MMP101E0170
Sales
Confirm pricing method: $0.55940/LB on 48,000 LB = $26,851.20 — pricing is per pound (LB), not per piece or per CWT — set LB pricing correctly in Invex
Sales
Ship Via: Best Way — Terms: NET 30 — ship to: 131 Harvester CT HWY 24, Anderson, SC 29626 (South Carolina — Anderson facility) — note PO originates from Ottawa, IL but ships to SC
Sales
Enter sales order — part# MMP101E0170 — description: 10MM HR PLATE 1E0170, 64″×189″ — 48,000 LB — $0.55940/LB — due 08/05/25 — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisition for 10MM HR plate — 64″×189″ (nearly 16 ft — confirm equipment can handle 189″ length) — grade 1E0170 — 48,000 LB total weight
Purchasing
Approve requisition
Purchasing
Convert requisition to PO — issue to mill
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt at FOB warehouse
Purchasing
Create receipt at FOB warehouse when material arrives
Purchasing
Create transfer to destination warehouse if applicable
Purchasing
Receive 48,000 LB — verify 10MM thickness, 64″×189″ dimensions, and grade 1E0170 — confirm actual received weight at receipt
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job set
Production Planning
Schedule & run production — due 08/05/25

Process plate to 64″×189″ — verify 10MM thickness — tag pieces with customer part# MMP101E0170 and PO# 75276 — confirm actual piece count and weight before staging

Move finished material to staging
Inventory
Create transport order — ship via Best Way to SMF, 131 Harvester CT HWY 24, Anderson, SC 29626
Sales
Stage & load — confirm total weight on truck against 48,000 LB expected — ship due 08/05/25
Shipping
Invoice at $0.55940/LB on actual shipped weight — Terms: NET 30 — reference PO# 75276 and part# MMP101E0170 on invoice — log invoice# in tracker

Exercise 9
Contract Sale entry — Interstate Sheet Metal Co Inc PO #0022951-00
Interstate Sheet Metal — 4 lines — TP100 abrasion-resistant plate — priced per EA/PC — mixed UOM — MTR required all items — FOB Delivered — due 03/11/26
Not started
▼
Exercise checklist
Review PO — 4-line order for TP100 abrasion-resistant specialty plate — TP100 is a high-strength, wear-resistant alloy (similar to AR100/T-1) — requires grade-specific sourcing; do not substitute standard A36 or A572 — PO date 3/4/26, due 3/11/26 (7-day lead time)
Sales
Note mixed unit of measure: Lines 001 and 004 use PC (pieces); Lines 002 and 003 use EA (each) — functionally the same per-piece pricing method — ensure the correct UOM is selected in Invex for each line
Sales
Note Line 001 cut size: 60″×100″ — non-standard (most other lines are 60″×120″) — enter 100″ length exactly as ordered — do not default to 120″
Sales
Note Line 004 part# notation: 3/8-TP100 5 X 10 = 5′×10′ = 60″×120″ — dimensions expressed in feet on the PO; enter as inches in Invex
Sales
Ship Via: DELIVERED (FOB Destination) — freight cost is your responsibility to the customer dock at 1515 ITC Way, Anniston, AL 36207 — factor freight into margin review
Sales
Terms: .5%10 N30 — customer earns 0.5% discount if paid within 10 days — note this on the sales order so invoicing is set up correctly
Sales
Note: MTR required on ALL line items — all raw material must be accompanied by MTR or equivalent — flag this requirement at order entry so it carries through to shipping
Sales
Enter Line 001: 1/4-TP100 60X100 — 22 PC — $331.83/PC — $7,300.26 — GL: 1380 — 60″×100″ non-standard length
Sales
Enter Line 002: 1/2-TP100 60X120 — 15 EA — $796.38/EA — $11,945.70 — GL: 1380
Sales
Enter Line 003: 5/16-100K-60X120 — 14 EA — $497.74/EA — $6,968.36 — GL: 1380 — TP100 specialty plate, 60″×120″
Sales
Enter Line 004: 3/8-TP100 5X10 (60″×120″) — 12 PC — $597.25/PC — $7,167.00 — GL: 1380 — enter dimensions as 60″×120″ in Invex
Sales
Vendor Code: STE987 — Buyer: Sherdena Bryant — Verify order total: $33,381.32 — log SO# in tracker
Sales
Create purchase requisitions for TP100 specialty plate — all four lines require TP100/AR100 grade — confirm sourcing availability for 7-day lead time — note L001 at 60″×100″ may require custom cut

Approve requisitions

Convert requisitions to POs — issue to mill or specialty plate supplier
Purchasing
Create pre-receipt, receipt, and transfer as needed
Purchasing
Receive all 63 total pieces — verify TP100 grade and cut size for each line — confirm 60″×100″ for L001 and 60″×120″ for L002–L004 — collect MTRs at receipt
Receiving
Assign material to order & create job sets
Production Planning
Schedule & run production — due 03/11/26
Job Scheduling
Process TP100 plate — verify cut sizes: L001 at 60″×100″, L002–L004 at 60″×120″ — tag all pieces — stage MTRs by line item
Production Recording
Move finished material to staging — group by line item
Inventory
Create transport order — DELIVERED terms — arrange freight to Interstate Sheet Metal Co Inc, 1515 ITC Way, Anniston, AL 36207 — freight is prepaid (your cost)
Sales
Stage & load all 63 pieces — count per line — include MTRs for all 4 lines with shipment — PO# 0022951-00 must appear on all documents
Shipping
Invoice shipment — Terms: .5%10 N30 — PO# 0022951-00 on invoice — log invoice# in tracker

Unhappy Path Reference — All 47 Scenarios
Training mode Awareness mode shows context + resolution guidance for discussion. Practice mode adds resolution checklists for hands-on training.

Read the blanket release record below. Click any field you think contains an error. When you’re done, click Check Answers.
Department Sort — Day 3 Contract Sale
Drag each step into the department responsible for it. On mobile: tap a step to select it (highlighted), then tap a column to place it. Click Check Answers when all steps are placed.

End of Day Debrief
★ Facilitator notes — Debrief (12:30–1:00)
  • Export CSVs from the process tracker — review elapsed times, highlight any steps that required rework or correction
  • Discuss the distinction: when do you create a source order vs. a release to produce? Ensure all trainees can articulate the difference
  • Review 2–3 unhappy paths focused on blanket/contract and production scenarios
  • Log any open system issues or questions for IT / project team follow-up
  • Preview Day 4: Toll Sale — customer-owned coil processing
All 3 scenarios attempted by each trainee
Process tracker CSVs exported and saved
Release to pick vs. release to produce distinction confirmed — questions answered
2–3 unhappy paths discussed as a group
Open questions documented and sent to project team
Day 4 topics previewed (Toll Sale)
Trainee feedback collected

All Process Maps ►
Intro
8:00–8:30
Non-Specific
8:30–10:00
Break
10:00–10:15
Specific
10:15–12:00
Debrief
12:00–12:30

Toll Sale — Customer-Owned Coil Processing

Today you will practice the toll sale process: creating pre-receipts for incoming customer-owned coils, building toll production orders with specific processing instructions, recording production, and invoicing for processing charges only (not material).

2
Scenarios
4h
Session
47
Unhappy Paths
6
Process Levels
Learning objectives
→Understand the difference between non-specific and specific toll processing
→Create a pre-receipt for an incoming customer-owned coil
→Create a toll production order and configure processing instructions from a customer PO
→Record production for a toll job with specific cut, handling, and tagging requirements
→Create a transport and invoice for processing charges only (not material cost)
★ Facilitator notes — Intro (8:00–8:30)
  • Walk through the Toll Sale process map (L2) on screen before starting scenarios
  • Emphasize that in a toll sale, Steel Warehouse never owns the material — the customer coil must be tracked separately from owned inventory at all times
  • Explain non-specific toll (ad hoc processing job) vs. specific toll (tied to a Valmont-style blanket framework)
  • Confirm everyone is logged into Invex and has access to toll order entry before starting
Day 4 Scenarios
Non-Specific Toll
Temper pass leveling — Vision Metals
Vision Metals, LLC
Not started
▼
Customer-owned coil (PO #21882)
  • HR .371" × 48.03" × Coil — 48,440 lbs — A36 VTAG#C0002567
Processing instructions
  • Temper Pass Leveling — coil > .350" thick, run 1st SFT
  • Cut 40 pcs @ 120" — cut balance @ 96"
  • Crossband all sheet lifts — ship all drops > 48"
  • 5,500# max lift — sheet lifter & fork side
Tolerances & tagging
  • Gauge: .360–.385 — Length: −0/+.50" — Width: ≥ 48.00"
  • Flatness: < .25" every 2 ft.
  • Tag finished material as HR 3/8" × 48"
Invoice
  • 48,440# × $3.00/CWT = $1,453.20 (processing only — no material charge)
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Toll Sale ↗ Pre-Receipt ↗ Toll Order ↗ Transport
Step checklist
Review Vision Metals PO #21882 — confirm processing instructions
Sales
Create pre-receipt in Invex — VTAG#C0002567, 48,440# HR .371" × 48.03", A36
Sales
Customer coil arrives — verify coil tag against pre-receipt — log as customer-owned toll material
Receiving
Review toll SO setup — confirm customer coil is assigned and received — release job to production
Purchasing
Create toll production order — Temper Pass Leveling route
Production Planning
Enter processing instructions: run 1st SFT (coil > .350" thick)
Production Planning
Schedule production job
Job Scheduling
Run production: cut 40 pcs @ 120", cut balance @ 96"
Production Recording
Crossband all sheet lifts — set aside all drops > 48" for shipment
Production Recording
Verify tolerances — gauge, length, width, flatness
Production Recording
Tag finished material as HR 3/8" × 48"
Production Recording
Move finished material to staging/storage area
Inventory
Create transport — our truck to Chattanooga (Vision Metals pickup)
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled pickup
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date
Shipping
Invoice processing charge — 48,440# @ $3.00/CWT = $1,453.20
Sales

Specific Toll
Multi-length plate processing — Valmont
Valmont Industries
Not started
▼
Line items — PO #689341 (due 4/10/26)
  • RP05262 — PLT 5/16" × 60" × 400" — 2 pcs $98.91 ea
  • RP05268 — PLT 5/16" × 60" × 520" — 8 pcs $128.58 ea
  • RP05275 — PLT 5/16" × 60" × 662" — 4 pcs $163.69 ea
Key difference from non-specific toll
  • Specific toll is tied to a Valmont blanket framework in Invex — pre-receipt must link to that order
  • All pieces must be tagged with Valmont part numbers (RP05262 / RP05268 / RP05275)
  • Cut instructions and tolerances come from the Valmont PO, not a separate CPS
  • Invoice is for processing only — 14 pcs total, $1,881.22
Process maps
Open ↗ Order Mgmt ↗ Toll Sale ↗ Pre-Receipt ↗ Toll Order ↗ Transport
Step checklist
Review Valmont PO #689341 — confirm part numbers, lengths, and quantities
Sales
Create pre-receipt in Invex — link to Valmont toll blanket agreement
Sales
Customer coil(s) arrive — verify coil tag against pre-receipt — log as customer-owned toll material
Receiving
Review toll SO setup — confirm customer coil is assigned and received — release job to production
Purchasing
Create internal production order — apply Valmont part-specific cut instructions
Production Planning
Schedule production job
Job Scheduling
Cut RP05262: 2 pcs @ 400" (5/16" × 60")
Production Recording
Cut RP05268: 8 pcs @ 520" (5/16" × 60")
Production Recording
Cut RP05275: 4 pcs @ 662" (5/16" × 60")
Production Recording
Tag all pieces with Valmont part numbers (RP05262 / RP05268 / RP05275)
Production Recording
Move finished material to staging/storage area
Inventory
Create transport — truck to 15202 E Admiral Pl, Tulsa, OK 74116 — due 4/10/26
Sales
Stage finished goods day prior to scheduled shipment
Shipping
Load & ship material on transport date
Shipping
Invoice processing charge — 14 pcs total, $1,881.22 (no material cost)

Supplemental Exercises — Extra Practice
Exercises 1–5 are non-specific toll orders — Steel Warehouse processes customer-owned material (slit, level, CTL, temper pass, or blast) for a processing fee only. Exercises 6–10 are specific toll orders from Valmont Industries — Valmont owns material cut to exact customer part specifications and exported to their Mexico manufacturing plant. All exercises: processing fee only — no material purchase.
Non-Specific Toll

Non-Specific Toll
Temper pass & CTL exercise 5 — Peak Metals #NUAL-TA26358/1
Peak Metals Inc. — 1 coil (45,160 lbs) NuStrength Grade 100 / ASTM A 656 Gr100 — temper pass then CTL to .250″×60″×240″ — scrap OD lap + last 14′ of ID — ship FOB Chattanooga, TN — due ASAP
Not started
▼
  1. Receive and review the toll order. Peak Metals owns the coil (Coil No. 5535096 — 45,160 lbs); SWC is performing two operations on customer-owned material: (1) temper pass the full coil, then (2) cut-to-length. Because both operations are on the same toll order, confirm with Production that the coil is routed through the temper mill before the CTL line — wrong sequence wastes the coil.
  2. Understand the grade. NuStrength Grade 100 / ASTM A 656 Grade 100 / meets ASTM A 1018 Grade 100 is a high-strength low-alloy structural steel (minimum 100 ksi yield). Handle with care — no flame cutting, no torch straightening. Flag to Production that this is a premium grade and confirm your processing parameters are within spec for A 656 Gr 100 material.
  3. Enter the toll order in Invex (no material purchase). This is a processing fee only order. Steps:
    • Customer: Peak Metals Inc. (Glenview, IL)
    • PO reference: NUAL-TA26358/1  •  Ref# 26358
    • Order type: Non-Specific Toll — no material cost, no inventory entry for material value
    • Operation: Temper Pass + Cut-to-Length (dual operation — note both in the job description)
    • Spec: .250″ × 60″ × 240″
    • Qty: All (full coil yield — quantity confirmed after processing; see OD/ID scrap rule below)
    • Price: $5.55/cwt — estimated total $2,506.38 based on 45,160 lb coil weight
    • Terms: Net 30 days  •  FOB Chattanooga, TN
  4. Log receipt of customer-owned coil. When Coil No. 5535096 arrives at the plant, record it as customer-owned material received — it does not go into SWC inventory as a purchased item. Note the coil number, certified weight (45,160 lbs), and condition on receipt in the toll log.
  5. Purchasing review. Review toll SO setup — confirm customer-owned coil (Coil No. 5535096) is assigned and received — release job to production.
  6. Apply and communicate the OD/ID scrap rule. The PO specifies: “Scrap full OD lap and last 14′ of ID.” This is Peak Metals’ quality instruction — these coil ends are discarded, not invoiced. Production must tag and set aside scrap footage; actual invoiceable weight is the yield after OD lap and last 14′ ID are removed. Confirm final yield weight with Production before invoicing.
  7. Note the tolerance and flatness requirements.
    • Thickness tolerance: ±0.0083″ (tight — verify temper pass settings)
    • Length tolerance: −0 / +¼″ (no undercut; slight over is acceptable)
    • Flatness: max 0.375″ in any 3′ span — aim dead flat
    • Short piece: cut as long as possible (do not discard — cut the remnant as a long short piece)
  8. Prepare bundles and Bill of Lading per PO instructions.
    • Max bundle weight: 10,000 lbs per bundle
    • Dunnage: 2×4 spacers between layers
    • Unloading: forklift (standard)
    • B/L must show: “Peak Metals P.O. 26358” plus pieces and weight per bundle — these two items are mandatory on the Bill of Lading
  9. Stage finished goods day prior to transport. Confirm bundles are complete with 2×4 spacers between layers — max 10,000 lbs per bundle — verify B/L is prepared with required notations: “Peak Metals P.O. 26358” and pieces & weight per bundle.
  10. Load & ship FOB Chattanooga on transport date. Confirm carrier and pick-up with the shipping desk. Attach the B/L with all required notations before releasing to the carrier. Freight cost is Peak Metals’ responsibility (FOB origin — confirm against PO freight terms).
  11. Invoice Peak Metals for processing fee only. After confirming final yield weight (post-scrap), calculate the invoice:
    • Rate: $5.55/cwt — apply to actual invoiceable weight (not full coil weight if scrap removed)
    • Estimated total: $2,506.38 (based on 45,160 lbs — adjust if yield differs)
    • Invoice reflects processing fee only — no material charge
    • Terms: Net 30 days
    Do not invoice until actual yield weight is confirmed — the OD/ID scrap reduces the billable weight.
Peak Metals Inc.
2111 Chestnut Ave Suite 210 • Glenview, IL 60025
PURCHASE ORDER
PO# NUAL-TA26358/1  •  Ref: 26358
Date: 04/07/2026
Vendor (Processor)
Steel Warehouse Co., LLC
Chattanooga, TN
Bill To / Ship To
Peak Metals Inc.
2111 Chestnut Ave Suite 210
Glenview, IL 60025
Terms & Delivery
Net 30 days
FOB: Chattanooga, TN
Delivery: ASAP

/ A 1018 Gr100		 Temper Pass
then CTL
Scrap OD lap
+ last 14′ ID		 .250″×60″×240″
Qty: All (full yield)		 Thk: ±0.0083″
Lgth: −0/+¼″
Flat: 0.375″ max/3′
Aim: dead flat		 $5.55/cwt $2,506.38
B/L must show: “Peak Metals P.O. 26358” • pieces and weight per bundle  —  Bundles: max 10,000 lbs • 2×4 spacers  —  Short piece: cut as long as possible  —  Processing fee only (customer-owned material)
Payment: Net 30 days  •  FOB: Chattanooga, TN  •  PO# on all shipping docs
Est. Total: $2,506.38
Exercises 6–10 — Specific Toll (Valmont Industries, Chattanooga): Valmont owns the plate material; SWC cuts it to exact Valmont part specifications (RP0xxxx part numbers) and ships to Valmont’s manufacturing plant in Apodaca, Nuevo Leon, Mexico. Key differences from non-specific toll: material is traced by Valmont part number, finished pieces are labeled with Valmont part numbers, and every shipment requires export documentation (USMCA certificate of origin, commercial invoice) for Mexico delivery.
Specific Toll
CTL exercise 6 — Valmont Industries #694054-4

Unhappy Path Reference — All 47 Scenarios
Training mode Awareness mode shows context + resolution guidance for discussion. Practice mode adds resolution checklists for hands-on training.

Spot the Error — Day 4 Toll Processing
Review the toll processing order below. Click any field you think contains an error. When you’re done, click Check Answers.
Department Sort — Day 4 Toll Processing
Drag each step into the department responsible for it. On mobile: tap a step to select it (highlighted), then tap a column to place it. Click Check Answers when all steps are placed.
Term Blaster — Day 4 Toll / Cross-Border

End of Day Debrief
★ Facilitator notes — Debrief (12:00–12:30)
  • Export CSVs from the process tracker — review elapsed times, pay attention to steps involving pre-receipt and production recording where trainees are most likely to get stuck
  • Discuss: what is different about invoicing a toll order vs. a spot or contract sale? (processing charge only — no material)
  • Review 2–3 unhappy paths focused on Toll scenarios — especially coil discrepancies and drop rule issues
  • Log any open system issues or questions for IT / project team follow-up
  • Preview Day 5: Transports & Invoicing — end-to-end shipping and billing process
Both scenarios attempted by each trainee
Process tracker CSVs exported and saved
Non-specific vs. specific toll distinction confirmed — questions answered
2–3 unhappy paths (Toll-focused) discussed as a group
Open questions documented and sent to project team
Day 5 topics previewed (Transports & Invoicing)
Trainee feedback collected

12:15–1:00

Supplemental Exercises — Practice & Exploration

Today is unstructured practice time. You have already completed end-to-end scenarios for Spot Sale, Contract Sale, and Toll Sale. Today is about building confidence in the system: looking things up, handling exceptions, and exploring areas you want to understand better.

5
Lookup Exercises
5
Orders to Trace
47
Unhappy Paths
Open
Q&A
Today's goals
→Look up raw and finished goods inventory for 5 specific products
→Trace 5 orders through the system and record key data points
→Practice working through unhappy paths in the system, not just discussing them
→Make a forecasting change and understand its downstream impact
→Review key reports with your facilitator and understand what to look for
★ Facilitator notes — Intro / Recap (8:00–8:30)
  • Open the floor: what were the hardest steps from Days 1–4? Note these — they will drive the Q&A session at the end
  • Today is self-paced — some trainees may need more time on certain exercises than others; that is expected
  • Circulate during stock lookups and order status exercises — these are often where navigation gaps surface
  • For unhappy path practice, encourage each trainee to pick at least 2–3 they personally struggled to understand
Quality Exercises — Returns & Rejections
Work through each quality scenario below in Invex. Complete all steps in the system, then record your results and observations in the notes section for each exercise.
Exercise 1 Vendor Return Quality

Process a return of non-conforming raw material back to the vendor. Locate a received coil, initiate the vendor return in Invex, and complete all required documentation and system steps.

Coil / Tag #
Vendor Name
Return Reason
Return Authorization #
Steps completed
Results & Notes
Exercise 2 Customer Return Quality

Process a customer return (RMA) for material sent back from a customer. Record the return in Invex, assign a disposition (return to stock, scrap, or rework), and complete the required credit or adjustment steps.

Original SO #
Customer Name
Return Reason
Disposition
Steps completed
Results & Notes
Exercise 3 Internal Rejection Quality

Record an internal rejection for material that fails inspection during production. Place the material on hold in Invex, document the non-conformance, and complete the disposition decision — scrap, rework, or downgrade.

Tag # / Material
Production Job #
Non-conformance Type
Disposition
Steps completed
Results & Notes
Exercise 1 — Stock & Inventory Lookup
For each product below, look it up in Invex and record the current on-hand quantities for both raw material (coil) and finished goods (FG) inventory. Use actual system values — do not estimate.
★ Facilitator notes — Stock Lookups (8:30–9:30)
  • Walk trainees through the inventory inquiry screen for the first item as a group, then let them complete the rest independently
  • Remind trainees that raw inventory shows coil weight in stock, and FG inventory shows processed and tagged material available to ship
  • If quantities are zero for a product, that is a valid answer — have them note it and discuss why
# Product Raw Inventory (lbs) FG Inventory (lbs) Notes
1 1/4" HRPO 60×120
2 3/8" HR 60×144
3 3/4" HR 60×120
4 12 Gauge HRPO 72×120
5 7 Gauge HRPO 60×72
Group observations / discussion notes
Exercise 2 — Order Status Inquiry
Look up 5 orders in Invex — use orders from your training scenarios or orders assigned by your facilitator. For each order, trace it through the system and record the information below.
★ Facilitator notes — Order Status (9:45–10:30)
  • Assign specific order numbers from the training environment, or let trainees use orders they created during Days 2–4
  • The profit margin % field is especially useful — make sure trainees understand where to find selling price vs. cost in the system
  • If trainees cannot find a field (e.g. value-added amount), use it as a teaching moment to show where that data lives

Stage of Process

Exercise 3 — Unhappy Path Practice
Pick unhappy paths you want to work through in the system — not just discuss. Use the filters to narrow by process area. Practice mode is always on here so you can work through the resolution steps and take notes. Aim for at least 3.
★ Facilitator notes — Unhappy Path Practice (10:30–11:15)
  • Encourage each trainee to pick paths that were genuinely confusing during Days 2–4, not ones they already feel confident about
  • For each path they practice, have them talk through their resolution out loud — hearing it reinforces it better than reading
  • If a trainee picks a path that requires system access they don't have, use it as a discussion item instead
212 of 212
Spin the Wheel
Select a specific tag above (not “All”) to load the wheel
Exercise 4 — Forecasting Changes
Customer forecasts drive planning in Invex. When a customer updates their expected demand, that change needs to be reflected in the system so purchasing and production can plan accordingly. Practice making a forecast change and observe the downstream effect.
★ Facilitator notes — Forecasting (11:15–11:45)
  • Walk through where forecasts live in Invex and how they are structured before trainees start the exercise
  • Have trainees reduce a forecast first (safer), then increase one and observe the planning impact
  • Key discussion point: what happens to open source orders and releases when a forecast is reduced below what is already committed?
  • If time allows: show how a forecast change surfaces in the purchasing plan or production schedule
Forecast exercise — complete 3 changes

For each customer below, find their active forecast in Invex, record the current quantity, make the specified change, and note the downstream impact you observe.

Change 1 — Forecast Reduction
Customer
Product / Item
Forecast Period
Current Forecast Qty (lbs)
New Forecast Qty (lbs)
Reason for Change
Downstream Impact Observed
Change 2 — Forecast Increase
Customer
Product / Item
Forecast Period
Current Forecast Qty (lbs)
New Forecast Qty (lbs)
Reason for Change
Downstream Impact Observed
Change 3 — Free Choice
Customer
Product / Item
Forecast Period
Current Forecast Qty (lbs)
New Forecast Qty (lbs)
Type of Change
What you observed & learned
Exercise 5 — Reporting
Facilitator-led. Your trainer will walk through the reports most relevant to your functional group. Use the notes fields below to record what each report shows, where to find it, and when you would use it day-to-day.
★ Facilitator notes — Reporting (11:45–12:15)
  • Choose reports based on the functional groups in the room — a sales-heavy group will need different reports than a production-heavy group
  • Show each report live in the system, not just as a screenshot — trainees need to know how to run it themselves
  • For each report, explain: what question does this answer, how often should you run it, and what action does it drive?
  • Common starting points: open order report, inventory valuation, production status, aging AR, on-time shipment

Promapp Process Maps

All department process maps in one searchable place. Filter by department or search by name to open the Promapp process you need.

32 of 32
Q&A & Catch-Up
★ Facilitator notes — Q&A / Wrap-up (12:15–1:00)
  • Open the floor: what is still unclear? What do you wish you had more time on?
  • Work through any items from the “questions to follow up” log below that have answers — parking lot the rest with an owner and due date
  • Close with a confidence check: on a scale of 1–5, how confident are you running your part of a spot sale / contract sale / toll sale independently?
  • Send a summary of open items to the project team within 24 hours of training
Questions log

Log questions as they come up throughout the day. Mark them resolved as answers are found.

Answer / Resolution
Follow-up owner
Confidence check — end of training week

Rate your confidence running each process independently (1 = not confident, 5 = fully confident). Be honest — this helps identify where more practice is needed.

Process
